vision_profile.current_state: You are a beginner eager to start your career in AI, with foundational knowledge but lacking practical, industry-ready skills.
vision_profile.main_blocker: Your biggest obstacle is translating theoretical understanding into deployable code and real-world project experience.
vision_profile.top_motivation: You are motivated to secure your first Python Intern role and build a strong foundation for a future in AI/ML.

milestones.label: M01
milestones.o: You can write clean, efficient Python scripts, understand core data structures, and apply basic OOP principles to solve programming problems.
milestones.identity_statement: You are building the essential programming bedrock, transforming into a confident Python scripter ready for technical challenges.

project.title: CLI Contact Book Application
project.description: Develop a command-line interface (CLI) application to manage contacts, including adding, viewing, updating, and deleting entries. Implement data persistence using a simple file format (e.g., CSV or JSON).
project.deliverable: A functional Python CLI application with a clear README, unit tests for core functionalities, and a sample data file.
seeded_errors: The initial implementation has a bug where deleting a contact by name deletes all contacts with similar substrings.
The data loading function doesn't handle empty files gracefully, leading to a crash on first run.

video.title: Reading and Writing Files in Python

science.desc: A colleague's Python script for data processing is throwing 'FileNotFoundError' intermittently. Analyze the code and suggest robust error handling strategies.

modules.title: Algorithms & Data Handling
lessons: Introduction to common data structures (Stacks, Queues, Linked Lists)

science.desc: Explain the difference between a list and a tuple in Python, and when you would choose one over the other.

milestones.label: M02
milestones.o: You can effectively wrangle, clean, and visualize data using popular Python libraries, and collaborate on code using Git and GitHub.
milestones.identity_statement: You are becoming a proficient data handler and team player, ready to contribute to real-world data projects.

project.title: Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) Report
project.description: Perform an EDA on a public dataset (e.g., Kaggle dataset on housing prices or customer churn). Clean the data, handle missing values, identify outliers, and generate insightful visualizations. Document your findings in a Jupyter Notebook.
project.deliverable: A Jupyter Notebook with clean, commented code, visualizations, and a summary of key insights. The notebook should be version-controlled using Git.
seeded_errors: The initial data cleaning script incorrectly imputes missing categorical values with the mean, instead of the mode.
A plot generated has an incorrect x-axis label, making it misleading.

science.desc: You've received a dataset with inconsistent date formats and several columns containing 'N/A' or empty strings. Outline your approach to clean and standardize this data.

modules.title: Data Visualization & Collaboration Tools
lessons: Creating various plot types (scatter, bar, line, histogram)
Basic Git commands (clone, add, commit, push, pull)

video.title: Effective Pull Requests and Code Reviews

science.desc: Describe a situation where you used Git to collaborate on a project. What challenges did you face, and how did you overcome them?

milestones.label: M03
milestones.o: You understand fundamental machine learning concepts, can implement basic supervised learning algorithms, and evaluate model performance.
milestones.identity_statement: You are transitioning from a Python scripter to an aspiring ML practitioner, grasping the core principles that drive intelligent systems.

project.title: Predictive Model for Customer Churn
project.description: Build a classification model to predict customer churn using a provided dataset. Preprocess the data, train at least two different supervised learning models (e.g., Logistic Regression, Decision Tree), evaluate their performance, and select the best one.
project.deliverable: A Jupyter Notebook demonstrating data preprocessing, model training, evaluation metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score), and a conclusion on the best model. Include visualizations of model performance.
seeded_errors: The model evaluation is performed on the training data, leading to an overoptimistic assessment of performance.
Categorical features are one-hot encoded without handling potential high cardinality, leading to a very wide dataset.

video.title: Vectors and Matrices in Machine Learning

science.desc: A model's loss function is not converging. You suspect the learning rate is either too high or too low. How would you diagnose and address this using your understanding of gradients?

modules.title: Supervised Learning Fundamentals
lessons: Model evaluation metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1, RMSE)

science.desc: You've trained a classification model with 95% accuracy. Is this always a good metric? When might it be misleading, and what other metrics would you consider?

milestones.label: M04
milestones.o: You can apply feature engineering techniques, build robust ML pipelines, and deploy simple models as APIs for inference.
milestones.identity_statement: You are moving beyond theoretical ML, gaining the practical skills to prepare data for models and make them accessible through basic APIs.

project.title: Simple ML Model API with Flask/FastAPI
project.description: Take the best model from your M03 project (customer churn prediction) and wrap it in a simple REST API using Flask or FastAPI. The API should accept new customer data as input and return a churn prediction.
project.deliverable: A Python application with a working API endpoint for predictions, a `requirements.txt` file, and instructions for running the API locally. Include basic error handling for invalid inputs.
seeded_errors: The API endpoint expects raw categorical data, but the model was trained on one-hot encoded features, leading to prediction errors.
The API crashes if a required input field is missing from the request payload.

video.title: Transforming Raw Data into Powerful Features

science.desc: You are building a fraud detection model where only 1% of transactions are fraudulent. Your initial model has high accuracy but misses most fraud cases. How would you adjust your feature engineering and evaluation strategy?

video.title: Building a Prediction API with Flask

science.desc: You've built a model that predicts house prices. How would you expose this model to other applications using a REST API? What are the key components you'd need?

milestones.label: M05
milestones.o: You grasp the basics of neural networks, can implement simple deep learning models, and apply advanced OOP patterns for more maintainable code.
milestones.identity_statement: You are expanding your toolkit into the exciting realm of Deep Learning, while simultaneously refining your Python engineering skills for robust and scalable solutions.

project.title: Image Classifier with a Simple Neural Network
project.description: Build a simple Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) using TensorFlow/Keras to classify images from a small dataset (e.g., MNIST, Fashion MNIST, or CIFAR-10). Train the model, evaluate its performance, and visualize predictions.
project.deliverable: A Jupyter Notebook with the CNN model definition, training loop, evaluation, and visualizations of training history and sample predictions. Ensure the code is well-structured and follows good OOP practices where applicable.
seeded_errors: The model's final layer activation function is incorrect for multi-class classification, leading to poor performance.
The data augmentation pipeline is applied inconsistently between training and validation sets.

video.title: The Building Blocks of Deep Learning

science.desc: Your simple neural network is performing poorly on a classification task. You suspect it's not learning complex patterns. What architectural changes would you consider, and why?

modules.title: OOP & SOLID Principles
lessons: Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, Dependency Inversion

science.desc: Explain the Open/Closed Principle and provide an example of how you would apply it in a Python class design.

milestones.label: M06
milestones.o: You can containerize applications with Docker, understand basic cloud concepts, and deploy simple ML services to cloud platforms.
milestones.identity_statement: You are expanding your reach to the cloud, learning to package and deploy your intelligent applications for broader accessibility and scalability.

project.title: Dockerized ML Prediction Service
project.description: Containerize your ML prediction API (from M04) using Docker. Create a Dockerfile, build an image, and run the container locally. Ensure the API is accessible from outside the container.
project.deliverable: A Dockerfile, a `requirements.txt`, and a `main.py` (or similar) for your API. Instructions for building and running the Docker image. The API should be functional within the container.
seeded_errors: The Dockerfile copies the entire project directory, including large datasets, making the image unnecessarily large.
The Docker container fails to start because the port mapping is incorrect, preventing external access to the API.

modules.title: Cloud & Deployment Basics
lessons: Introduction to cloud computing (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS)
Key cloud providers (AWS, Azure, GCP) overview
Basic cloud deployment strategies for ML models

video.title: Deploying a Dockerized ML App to a Cloud VM

science.desc: Your Dockerized ML service works perfectly locally but fails to start on a cloud VM. What are the common reasons for this, and how would you debug it?

milestones.label: M07
milestones.o: You can independently design, implement, and deploy a complete AI/ML project, and confidently articulate your skills in technical interviews.
milestones.identity_statement: You are a capable and confident Python Intern, equipped with a portfolio of projects and the communication skills to launch your career in AI/ML.

project.title: End-to-End AI/ML Project: Recommendation System
project.description: Design and implement a simple content-based or collaborative filtering recommendation system. This involves data collection/preparation, model training, and exposing recommendations via a simple API. Document the entire process, including design choices and challenges.
project.deliverable: A complete, version-controlled project repository including data processing scripts, model training code, a prediction API (Dockerized), a comprehensive README, and a presentation summarizing your work.
seeded_errors: The recommendation system suffers from a 'cold start' problem for new users, providing generic recommendations.
The API's response time is too slow for real-time recommendations due to inefficient data retrieval.

modules.title: Capstone & Interview Readiness
lessons: Debugging & testing strategies for complex systems
Resume & portfolio building for AI roles

science.desc: Walk me through a challenging technical problem you've solved. What was the problem, your approach, and what did you learn?
